rationales.self_direction: He imagines 'pivot[ing] my thesis entirely' toward the idea that makes his 'heart... racing,' but then opens 'the narrowed proposal draft' because of his advisor's and family's expectations.
rationales.stimulation: The bigger thesis idea gives him a 'rush of clarity' and adrenaline, centered on a more ambitious and less predictable research direction.
rationales.conformity: He reins himself in because contradicting Keane and disappointing 'everyone back home' would 'prove them right,' so he defaults to the more expected path.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the 'stable salary, benefits' job because the thought of office work she does not care about creates 'this weight in my chest,' and she chooses freelance work instead.
rationales.security: With 'rent... due next week' and income that 'never quite adds up,' turning down the safer full-time role undermines stability and predictability.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps driving and even hides the earnings because 'the secret is the only way the work stays mine,' showing a strong need to preserve control over his own role.
rationales.hedonism: He drives 'until midnight' despite saying his back gives him trouble and rejecting rest, choosing strain over comfort.
rationales.tradition: He frames his choice through inherited expectations: 'My father worked until the week before he died. His father the same. A man provides.'
When asked about Jordan, he talks about 'Amman in a way I had not talked about it in months,' reconnecting with his cultural roots and past.
rationales.benevolence: He says yes to lunch 'because he rarely asks,' showing he is prioritizing closeness with his son and cherishing that relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He is moved by Tom caring about a project 'for no reason other than he wanted to do it,' then goes to the garage and reconnects with the part of himself that used to build things by choice.
rationales.tradition: He seeks out 'my dad's old chisels' still wrapped in oilcloth and sits holding one, drawing meaning from a family craft handed down from the past.

rationales.security: He advances Mahmoud's paycheck 'without thinking' even though the accountant 'has a point about cash flow,' putting financial stability at risk.
rationales.benevolence: He immediately says yes because Mahmoud's sister needs surgery and asks, 'what am I supposed to do, pretend I do not know the man's family?' showing concrete care for someone close to his circle.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes to the refugee family's placement 'before checking with HR' and deletes the succession-planning email, acting on his own judgment instead of institutional direction.
rationales.hedonism: He says these hard moments are why he still comes to work at fifty-eight 'instead of sitting on some beach,' explicitly choosing duty over ease and comfort.
rationales.security: Committing to openings 'before checking with HR' and refusing to engage succession planning both push against predictability and organizational stability.
rationales.conformity: He bypasses normal process by approving the placement before checking with HR and ignores Nadia's succession-planning email entirely.
rationales.benevolence: He gave Fatima time off while her mother was in hospice, and her gratitude highlights his ongoing care for people close to him at work.
rationales.universalism: He agrees to help 'a family from Sudan' because he remembers arriving with 'two suitcases and a cousin's phone number' and refuses to 'close that door' on others in need.
